howItWorks.title: How Unaria works
howItWorks.subtitle: A transparent, sustainable model focused on maximum social impact
howItWorks.step1Desc: Members contribute monthly fees from €6. Every cent is recorded and audited.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Unaria covers only the minimum operating costs strictly necessary to function.
howItWorks.step3Desc: The surplus is directed to accredited NGOs and our own direct action projects in critical zones, generating direct and verifiable impact.

transparency.title: Transparency commitment
transparency.subtitle: We publish our accounts every year. Every euro is traceable.

ngos.title: Our partner NGOs
ngos.subtitle: We work with accredited, audited organisations with maximum social impact
ngos.cruzRojaDesc: Spain's leading humanitarian organisation, active in emergencies and social action.
ngos.placeholderDesc: We are in the process of selecting new partner NGOs based on criteria of impact, transparency and efficiency.

testimonials.title: What our members say
testimonials.t1Text: Finally an association that explains exactly where every euro goes. Unaria's transparency convinced me from the very first moment.
testimonials.t2Text: With €10 a month I feel I'm making a real contribution. The model is smart: minimum costs, maximum impact.
testimonials.t3Text: I love being able to choose my fee and knowing it reaches trusted NGOs. I would recommend Unaria to everyone.

about.title: About us
about.missionText: Unaria is born from the conviction that organised solidarity is more efficient than individual charity. We act on two fronts: we channel funds towards the highest-impact NGOs and develop our own direct action projects in areas with documented vulnerability. We are the bridge between citizens' generosity and real change on the ground.
about.v1: Absolute transparency in accounts and management
about.v3: Rigour in selecting NGOs and our own projects
about.f1Bio: Founder and driving force behind Unaria. His vision and leadership have made it possible to build a transparent, impact-focused association. He believes that organised solidarity is the most efficient way to help, and works every day to make that a reality.
about.f2Bio: Co-founder of Unaria. With strong organisational skills and a genuine vocation for service, she oversees administrative management and internal communications. Her commitment ensures the association operates with rigour and transparency at all times.
about.f3Bio: Industrial engineer and co-founder of Unaria. His technical and analytical background is key to ensuring rigorous financial management, fund control and the long-term economic sustainability of the project.
about.legalText: Unaria is a non-profit association constituted under Law 4/2008, of 24 April, of Book Three of the Civil Code of Catalonia, relating to legal persons. Registered with the Associations Registry of the Generalitat de Catalunya.

howWeWorkPage.title: How we work
howWeWorkPage.subtitle: Our model is simple, honest and oriented towards maximum impact
howWeWorkPage.intro: When you become a member of Unaria, you join the association and contribute to a common fund. Unaria directs these funds in two directions: donations to accredited partner NGOs and funding for its own direct action projects in critical areas. This ensures efficiency, traceability and maximum impact.
howWeWorkPage.step1: Become a member and choose your monthly fee
howWeWorkPage.step1Detail: From €6/month. You can pay by SEPA direct debit or card. You can cancel at any time.
howWeWorkPage.step2Detail: All income accumulates in a dedicated bank account. We cover only the minimum essential costs (hosting, bank charges, accountancy).
howWeWorkPage.step3Detail: Every quarter we transfer the surplus: part to selected partner NGOs and part to our own projects. We publish everything: amounts, dates and bank references.
howWeWorkPage.importantTitle: Important: you are a member of Unaria, not of Cruz Roja
howWeWorkPage.importantText: The contractual relationship is between you and Unaria. Unaria, as an entity, makes donations to partner NGOs and funds its own projects. This has important legal and tax implications explained in our legal notice.
howWeWorkPage.ctaText: From €6/month you become part of a solidarity movement with real, transparent impact.

strategyPage.title: Our strategy
strategyPage.subtitle: The strategic pillars that guide our social action
strategyPage.intro: Unaria's approach is based on three fundamental strategic pillars to maximize the impact of every donation.
strategyPage.pillar1Desc: We evaluate and select partner NGOs based on objective criteria of impact, financial efficiency, and transparency. We only collaborate with audited and highly recognized organizations.
strategyPage.pillar2Desc: By channeling multiple small contributions into a single fund, we gain operational efficiency and impact capacity. This allows us to proportionally reduce management costs.
strategyPage.pillar3Desc: Our commitment is to maintain a minimal structure. The board of directors is not remunerated and operating costs are reduced to the minimum necessary for operations and legal compliance.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.responsibleText: UNARIA, Non-profit association. Tax ID (CIF): (pending). Registered address: Barcelona, Catalonia. Email: unariabcn@gmail.com
privacy.purposeText: Personal data provided is used to: (a) manage membership and corresponding fees; (b) process one-time donations; (c) send communications related to Unaria's activities (where consent exists); (d) comply with legal and tax obligations. Unaria does NOT share your data with partner NGOs. The NGOs' relationship is solely and exclusively with Unaria as an entity.
privacy.legalBasisText: Performance of a contractual relationship (membership registration), compliance with legal obligations and, where applicable, the data subject's explicit consent.
privacy.retentionText: Data is retained for the duration of the membership and, subsequently, for legally required periods (minimum 5 years for accounting records).
privacy.rightsText: You may exercise your rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability and objection by writing to unariabcn@gmail.com, attaching a copy of your identity document. You have the right to lodge a complaint with the Spanish Data Protection Agency (www.aepd.es).
privacy.ibanText: Bank details (IBAN) are stored encrypted with AES-256 and are never transmitted in plaintext. They are used solely to process the authorised SEPA direct debit.
privacy.cookiesText: Unaria uses only essential technical cookies required for the website to function. You can configure your cookie preferences in the banner displayed when accessing the site.

legal.title: Legal Notice
legal.ownerText: UNARIA, Non-profit association. Tax ID (CIF): (pending). Registered address: Barcelona, Catalonia. Contact email: unariabcn@gmail.com
legal.conditionsText: Accessing and using this website implies full acceptance of these terms. Unaria reserves the right to modify website content without prior notice.
legal.ipText: All website content (texts, images, logos, design) is owned by Unaria or used with permission. Reproduction in whole or in part without express authorisation is prohibited.
legal.liabilityText: Unaria is not responsible for any damages arising from the use of the website, nor for information contained on third-party websites that may be accessed via links.
legal.jurisdictionText: These terms are governed by Spanish law. For any dispute, the parties submit to the courts of Barcelona, waiving any other jurisdiction that may apply.

cookie.title: Cookie usage
cookie.text: Unaria uses only essential technical cookies required for the website to function. No personal data is transmitted to third parties for advertising purposes.

ownProjectsPage.title: Own Projects
ownProjectsPage.subtitle: Future vision: direct action in zones of documented vulnerability
ownProjectsPage.heroDesc: Unaria goes beyond supporting external NGOs. Our vision is to act directly on the ground in areas with documented structural problems, deploying our own coordinated projects.
ownProjectsPage.criticalZonesSubtitle: Thailand sits at the centre of some of Southeast Asia's most serious human rights violations. Our on-the-ground knowledge allows us to plan specific, effective interventions.
ownProjectsPage.zone1Desc: Transit zones where hundreds of thousands of Myanmar migrants work under serious labour exploitation in textile and manufacturing factories. High risk for unaccompanied migrant minors with no access to education or legal protection.
ownProjectsPage.zone2Desc: The seafood fishing sector is notorious for systematic use of forced labour and human trafficking networks. Workers lured with false promises, confined on vessels for months with no way out or means of communication.
ownProjectsPage.zone3Desc: Child labour is endemic in sugar cane harvesting across Thailand. Children as young as 8 work shifts of up to 12 hours in physically dangerous conditions, deprived of their right to education.
ownProjectsPage.zone4Desc: Sexual exploitation and sex tourism particularly affect minors and vulnerable populations in major tourist areas. Well-documented exploitation networks reported by international bodies including the ILO and ECPAT.
ownProjectsPage.visionDesc: Thailand concentrates some of Asia's most thoroughly documented humanitarian challenges. Its position as a regional transit hub, combined with a massive tourism industry and structural demand for cheap labour, creates conditions for systematic exploitation. Unaria wants to be there directly.
ownProjectsPage.ctaTitle: Want to drive these projects forward?
ownProjectsPage.ctaDesc: Every contribution brings us closer to our goal of direct presence in zones of vulnerability. Become a member or make a donation to fund our on-the-ground expansion.
ownProjectsPage.metaDesc: Learn about Unaria's vision to act directly in zones of documented vulnerability in Thailand: Myanmar border, fishing industry, agricultural and urban zones.
